text: The video shows a serous ovarian cyst on the right side, measuring approximately ten to twelve centimeters, visible during the examination.
Cystectomy is a procedure which needs to be performed extremely meticulously especially for young women who need to preserve their fertility.
The surgeon performs gentle dissection to separate the ovarian cyst from the ovarian cortex, ensuring a bloodless procedure as the capsule is carefully stripped away.

The surgeon completes the stripping of the ovarian cyst from the cortex, using gentle traction and counter traction to achieve the remaining dissection, visibly separating the cyst from the surrounding tissue.
The surgeon considers rupturing the large serous ovarian cyst on the right side to debulk it, facilitating easier completion of the cystectomy procedure.
The next question would be how to take out this ovarian cyst.
The surgeon employs a ribbonization technique, cutting open the ovarian cyst wall into a long ribbon shape, revealing the internal structure of the cyst.
The surgeon uses a trocar to provide traction while employing scissors to assist in the dissection process, facilitating the separation of the ovarian cyst from the surrounding tissue.
The surgeon uses two ports to perform the ribbonization technique, cutting the ovarian cyst wall into a long ribbon and removing it from the peritoneal cavity.
